changes_from_previous: Added discriminating assertions (includes-test-specifications, documents-design-assumptions, separates-data-layer, specifies-component-props, separates-state-from-ui, specifies-type-definitions)
Improved SKILL.md with explicit guidance: brainstorming fallback, finer layer separation, test/type/props/error-mapping inclusion
Assertions split into baseline (both should pass) and discriminating (skill should add value)

notes: Discriminating assertions now successfully differentiate: with-skill scores 100% vs without-skill 58.3% on discriminating assertions.
Baseline assertions remain non-discriminating (both 100%) — confirming they measure floor quality correctly.
The skill's value is strongest on simple and medium features (discriminating: with 100% vs without 50%/25%).
For complex features (large), both configurations pass all assertions — Sonnet already produces high-quality decomposition for complex tasks.
This suggests the skill acts as a 'floor-raiser': ensuring consistent quality regardless of feature complexity.
includes-test-specifications is the single strongest discriminator: with-skill always passes, without-skill never passes for simple/medium.
separates-data-layer is no longer discriminating — without-skill also separates schema in iteration 2 (improvement from iteration 1).
With-skill takes ~42s longer and uses ~5k more tokens due to structured process, but produces consistently higher-quality output.
Large without-skill includes-test-specifications passed but with borderline evidence (no explicit test file paths, only framework mention).
